title: Basics of Anatomy
description: Blank and single-select assessment for Basic Anatomy & Physiology.

sections.title: Basics of Anatomy
sections.summary: Medical terminology and basic anatomy assessment.

questions.prompt: Hydro- in the term Hydronephrosis means _____________.
In medical terminology, a prefix is placed at the ___________ of a word.
A prefix mainly _______________ the meaning of a medical term.
Prefixes usually consist of ____________ or two syllables.
In the term Hydronephrosis, which part is the prefix? ______________.
The suffix -itis means _________________.
In medical terminology, a suffix is placed at the ______________ of a word.
The suffix -osis means ____________________ condition.
The suffix usually describes a ____________________ or procedure.
In the term Nephritis, which part is the suffix? _____________________.
The root word Nephr refers to which organ? __________________________.
A root word represents the _____________________ part of a medical term.
The root word forms the ________________________ of a medical term.
In the word Hydronephrosis, the root word is ____________________.
The root word indicates the ____________ part involved in the medical term.
Anatomy is the study of the ____________________________ of the body.
Physiology is the study of how the body ________________________.
The maintenance of a stable internal environment is called _____________.
The cranial cavity contains the ____________________.
The spinal cavity contains the ____________________________.
Which system brings oxygen into the body and removes carbon dioxide?
Which level of organization consists of groups of similar cells?
Which body cavity contains the lungs?
Which body position means lying flat on the back with the face upward?
Which quadrant of the abdomen contains the appendix?
